channel.title: GeoPulse — Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefs on geopolitics, geography, and world history.

item.title: US federal court hears new case against Trump tariffs
item.description: The case is to overturn the temporary tariffs that Trump imposed after the Supreme Court struck down his earlier ones. The case is to overturn the temporary tariffs that Trump imposed after the Supreme Court struck down his earlier ones. item.title: Hawaii’s Kilauea erupts, blasting lava sky-high
item.description: Lava shot into the air, illuminating the night sky, as Hawaii’s Kīlauea volcano erupted. Lava shot into the air, illuminating the night sky, as Hawaii’s Kīlauea volcano erupted. Hawaii’s Kilauea erupts, blasting lava sky-high Lava shot into the air, illuminating the night sky, as Hawaii’s Kīlauea volcano erupted. item.title: Palestinian shot dead during Israeli settler attack on occupied West Bank village
item.description: The killing came as ex-security chiefs warned "government sponsored Jewish terrorism" was out of control. The killing came as ex-security chiefs warned "government sponsored Jewish terrorism" was out of control. item.title: Prince Harry sued for defamation by charity Sentebale he co-founded
item.description: Prince Harry left the charity last year in an acrimonious dispute over how Sentebale was being managed. Prince Harry left the charity last year in an acrimonious dispute over how Sentebale was being managed. item.title: Starmer and Trump talked military options to reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: British PM Starmer said he and US President Donald Trump discussed “military capabilities” to reopen Hormuz. British PM Starmer said he and US President Donald Trump discussed “military capabilities” to reopen Hormuz. item.title: Israeli settlers turn Passover into celebration of ethnic cleansing
item.description: Palestinians displaced by settler violence confront loss of community as settlers celebrate their forced expulsions. Palestinians displaced by settler violence confront loss of community as settlers celebrate their forced expulsions. Israeli settlers turn Passover into celebration of ethnic cleansing Palestinians displaced by settler violence confront loss of community as settlers celebrate their forced expulsions. item.title: Chimpanzees in Uganda locked in vicious 'civil war', say researchers
item.description: The once close-knit community of Ngogo chimpanzees have been at loggerheads for the last eight years. The once close-knit community of Ngogo chimpanzees have been at loggerheads for the last eight years. item.title: Netanyahu removes Spain from Gaza coordination centre over ‘hostility’
item.description: Israel's Netanyahu accused Spain of waging a 'diplomatic war' against Israel. Israel's Netanyahu accused Spain of waging a 'diplomatic war' against Israel. item.title: JD Vance expects ‘positive’ US-Iran war talks as he departs for Pakistan
item.description: Vice president says Trump gave 'pretty clear guidelines' on talks, which will be productive if Iran does not 'play' US. item.title: In Peru’s presidential race, a pro-Trump frontrunner sinks in final stretch
item.description: Donald Trump has sought greater influence over Latin America, but in Peru's election, will a pro-Trump stance pay off? item.title: Potholes and progress: Mamdani reflects on 100 days as New York’s mayor
item.description: Mamdani's first 100 days marked by childcare reforms, pothole fixes and polarising debates over affordability policies. item.title: Man accused of coercing wife into sex with 120 men goes on trial in Sweden
item.description: The 61-year-old denies threatening physical violence and plying his wife with drugs to force her into having sex with strangers. The 61-year-old denies threatening physical violence and plying his wife with drugs to force her into having sex with strangers. item.title: Popular Tanzanian musician Matonya charged with rape in Kenya
item.description: The 43-year-old denies the charge and has been released on bail and had to surrender his passport. item.title: Russia and Ukraine agree to Orthodox Easter truce
item.description: Vladimir Putin announced the truce, which will last from Saturday afternoon through Easter Sunday. Vladimir Putin announced the truce, which will last from Saturday afternoon through Easter Sunday. item.title: German transgender far-right extremist arrested in Czech Republic
item.description: German media reports say Marla-Svenja Liebich used to be a member of a neo-Nazi group called Blood and Honour. item.title: Melania Trump denies ties to Jeffrey Epstein and urges hearing for survivors
item.description: She called rumours that Epstein introduced her to Donald Trump "mean-spirited attempts to defame my reputation". item.title: Taiwan opposition leader meets Xi Jinping in Beijing
item.description: Cheng Li-wun is the Kuomintang's first sitting leader to visit China in a decade. Cheng Li-wun is the Kuomintang's first sitting leader to visit China in a decade. item.title: Trump says Iran's handling of Strait of Hormuz is 'not the agreement we have'
item.description: The US president accused Iran of "doing a very poor job" with the waterway as Israel and Lebanon to begin peace talks. The US president accused Iran of "doing a very poor job" with the waterway as Israel and Lebanon to begin peace talks.
